summary: Professional student-focused expense tracker app (“Monet tracker”) to record daily expenses, show weekly/monthly totals with simple graphs, using straightforward calculations and no online payment features.
architectural_notes: No online payment processing features (explicitly excluded).
Use straightforward calculations for weekly/monthly totals (no complex budgeting algorithms).
Design and architecture should be compatible with FlutterFlow app patterns.

existing_features.title: Build the core app IA (information architecture) and navigation on the frontend with these screens: Dashboard (overview), Add Expense, Expense List (with filters), and Settings (lightweight). Use English for all user-facing text.
Create an Add Expense form that allows students to record a daily expense with amount, category, date picker (default today), and optional note; include basic validation and inline error messages.

Implement Expense List view with sorting and filtering: filter by week/month/custom date range, filter by category, and show per-item amount, category, date, and note preview; allow edit and delete actions.

Compute and display weekly and monthly totals using straightforward math (sum of amounts) and present them on the Dashboard and in reporting sections. Provide per-category breakdown totals for the selected period.
Add simple, lightweight graphs to the Dashboard for spending trends: (1) a weekly daily-spend bar chart and (2) a monthly spend trend line or bar chart; keep visuals minimal and readable.

Deliver a clean, professional UI/UX design system tailored for students, aligned with FlutterFlow-like patterns: card-based layout, clear typographic hierarchy, consistent spacing, accessible contrast, and minimal ornamentation. Avoid blue/purple as primary brand colors unless explicitly requested.

Provide a concise in-repo architecture document describing frontend modules (screens/components/state), backend actor API surface, data model, and calculation rules (weekly/monthly boundaries). Include UI/UX recommendations and rationale for key patterns.
Change the color used by the Dashboard 'Daily Spending' graph (weekly bar chart) to a different chart color token so it is visually distinct from the other chart.
Rename the app to "Monet Tracker" everywhere the app name is presented to the user (header brand, footer copyright, Settings/About copy, and the HTML document title).

Update the UI styling to look more professional and cohesive across the app shell and top-level screens (without adding new features): refine spacing, typography hierarchy, and subtle surface styling to match a polished finance-tracker aesthetic.

new_feature_requests.summary: Expense entry: record daily expenses with amount, category, date, optional note (no payment features)
Totals & reporting: compute and display weekly and monthly totals using straightforward math
Simple graphs dashboard: lightweight charts for spending trends (weekly/monthly)
UI/UX design system: clean, professional student-friendly look aligned with FlutterFlow patterns
App architecture: define clean modular architecture and data model suitable for FlutterFlow build

Implement expense data model and persistence in the Motoko single-actor backend, supporting CRUD for daily expense records with: amount (number), category (enum or string), date (ISO yyyy-mm-dd), optional note, and createdAt/updatedAt timestamps.
UI polish: update Daily Spend graph styling (use a different color) and refine overall look to be more professional
